node.title: SQL Joins
node.description: Combine rows from multiple tables using join operations.
expectedOutcomes: Explain when to use left joins

resources.title: SQL joins documentation
resources.summary: INNER JOIN returns rows with matching values in both tables. LEFT JOIN keeps all rows from the left table.

chunks.text: Use INNER JOIN to write queries that combine matching rows across two tables.
Use LEFT JOIN when you need every row from the left table and matching rows from the right table.

resources.title: SQL join examples
resources.summary: Practical SQL join examples for beginner database learners.

chunks.text: A join example should name both tables, the join key, and the expected output rows.
